title: Pertinent Negatives
description: Pre-specified expectations for thesis-level analyses. Forces 'what's the dog that didn't bark?' before investigating. Based on IC Indicators & Warnings (I&W) framework.

expected.description: What you'd expect to see if the hypothesis were true

implication.description: What does the presence/absence mean for the thesis?

claim_ref.description: Optional reference to a specific claim in the claims table (e.g., '#3')

examples.expected: Revenue growth >15% YoY if demand thesis is correct
examples.implication: Demand thesis weakened — growth was only 8%, below sector average

examples.expected: Insider buying if management believes in turnaround
examples.implication: No insider purchases in 12 months despite stated optimism — actions don't match words

examples.expected: Patent filing activity if R&D pipeline is real
examples.implication: 3 new patent applications in Q4 — consistent with R&D narrative
